mission.title: falcosidekick: Create Configurable Toggle To enable/disable `falco_events` prom metric
mission.description: Create Configurable Toggle To enable/disable `falco_events` prom metric. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current falcosidekick deployment
steps.description:
Verify your falcosidekick version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falcosidekick -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falcosidekick
helm list -n falcosidekick 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working falcosidekick installation.

steps.title: Review falcosidekick configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant falcosidekick configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n falcosidekick -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falcosidekick
kubectl get configmap -n falcosidekick -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=falcosidekick
```
**Motivation**

👋 Hi sidekick project! 

I'm not sure if this is bug or a feature request. Our instances of sidekick are periodically OOMing.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Create Configurable Toggle To enable/disable `falco_events`…
steps.description:
Hi,

Thank you for this detailed report. This is an old known issue with the prometheus metrics.

I agree the metric `falco_events` is not relevant anymore. Since the last versions of Falco, Falco exposes itself this metric with a Prom endpoint, before that, it required the `falco-exporter`
```yaml
falco_events{hostname="falco-rw2n7",k8s_ns_name="<namespace name here>",k8s_pod_name="<pod name here>-6c66cccf94-nbkvb",priority="Notice",rule="<rule name here>",source="syscall"} 1
falco_events{hostname="falco-rw2n7",k8s_ns_name="<namespace name here>",k8s_pod_name="<pod name here>-6c66cccf94-ngr75",priority="Notice",rule="<rule name here>",source="syscall"} 3
falco_events{hostname="falco-rw2n7",k8s_ns_name="<namespace name here>",k8s_pod_name="<pod name here>-6c66cccf94-nhjlk",priority="Notice",rule="<rule name here>",source="syscall"} 3
falco_events{hostname="falco-rw2n7",k8s_ns_name="<name
```

steps.title: Upgrade falcosidekick to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade falcosidekick:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade falcosidekick falcosidekick/falcosidekick --namespace falcosidekick
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falcosidekick
helm list -n falcosidekick
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falcosidekick -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falcosidekick
kubectl get events -n falcosidekick --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Create Configurable Toggle To enable/disable `falco_events`…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hi,

Thank you for this detailed report. This is an old known issue with the prometheus metrics.

I agree the metric `falco_events` is not relevant anymore. Since the last versions of Falco, Falco exposes itself this metric with a Prom endpoint, before that, it required the `falco-exporter` component which is now deprecated.
codeSnippets:
falco_events{hostname="falco-rw2n7",k8s_ns_name="<namespace name here>",k8s_pod_name="<pod name here>-6c66cccf94-nbkvb",priority="Notice",rule="<rule name here>",source="syscall"} 1
falco_events{hostname="falco-rw2n7",k8s_ns_name="<namespace name here>",k8s_pod_name="<pod name here>-6c66cccf94-ngr75",priority="Notice",rule="<rule name here>",source="syscall"} 3
falco_events{hostname="falco-rw2n7",k8s_ns_name="<namespace name here>",k8s_pod_name="<pod name here>-6c66cccf94-nhjlk",priority="Notice",rule="<rule name here>",source="syscall"} 3
falco_events{hostname="falco-rw2n7",k8s_ns_name="<namespace name here>",k8s_pod_name="<pod name here>-6c66cccf94-nmfct",priority="Notice",rule="<rule name here>",source="syscall"} 1
falco_events{hostname="falco-rw2n7",k8s_ns_name="<namespace name here>",

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with falcosidekick installed or the issue environment reproducible.
